description: Initial seeding of the Session 16 examples corpus. Pairs demonstrate principles that the source system itself articulates. Growth happens as attribution audits continue — see README.md.

pairs.not_this: We will endeavour to utilise the most appropriate methodology.
pairs.rationale: GOV.UK's plain-English canon: prefer 'use' over 'utilise', 'try' over 'endeavour'.

pairs.but_this: We couldn't sign you in. Try again.
pairs.rationale: Mailchimp voice: plain language + actionable recovery instead of jargon.

pairs.not_this: Failure to comply with the aforementioned stipulations may result in penalties.
pairs.but_this: If you don't follow these rules, you may be fined.
pairs.rationale: 18F: plain language even in compliance contexts — accessibility is a civic obligation.

pairs.source_section: Content Style Guide — Leading with value
pairs.not_this: As part of our ongoing commitment to improving the experience, we've added dark mode.
pairs.but_this: Dark mode is here. Toggle it in Settings.
pairs.rationale: Lead with the news, not the preamble.

pairs.not_this: When you change your password you will be signed out of all devices and will need to sign back in on each one using your new password within the next 24 hours.
pairs.but_this: Changing your password signs you out everywhere. Sign back in with the new password within 24 hours.
pairs.rationale: GOV.UK target: ≤25 words per sentence. Split at natural consequence boundaries.

pairs.not_this: The file was uploaded by the user.
pairs.rationale: Active voice puts the actor first; inverts passive construction.

pairs.source_section: Voice and tone — Direct and clear
pairs.rationale: Passive is acceptable in confirmations (the subject is the state, not the actor); Atlassian tightens further by dropping the auxiliary.

pairs.not_this: The user should complete the setup.
pairs.rationale: Talk to the person, not about them. Second person lands warmer.

pairs.but_this: That password didn't match. Try again.
pairs.rationale: Error-recovery voice: describe the failure state, not the user's mistake. Mailchimp's empathy principle in action.

pairs.not_this: Are you sure you want to proceed with this dangerous action?
pairs.but_this: Delete 'My Document' permanently? This can't be undone.
pairs.rationale: Apple HIG: name the specific object + the specific consequence. 'Dangerous action' is abstract; the actual outcome is what the user needs.
Apple HIG: prefer specific verbs over generic affirmatives, especially on destructive confirmations.
Material: name what comes next, not the act of proceeding.
Polaris: start with a verb that describes the specific action — 'Submit' is too generic.

pairs.not_this: Oops! Something went wrong! Please try again!
pairs.rationale: Mailchimp: exclamation points are earned in celebration, not piled in errors. Excessive punctuation undermines calm.
Primer: sentence case for headings. Title case feels corporate; sentence case reads quieter.
Google: sentence case for UI labels. Capitalize only proper nouns + first word.
Mailchimp: no terminal punctuation on headings. The layout already signals end-of-phrase.

pairs.source_section: Voice and tone — Don't be dismissive
pairs.not_this: Just enter your email and you're done.
pairs.rationale: Mailchimp: 'just' and 'simply' minimize effort the user might actually feel. Drop the filler.

pairs.source_section: Voice and tone — Respect the user
pairs.not_this: Just re-enter your password to continue.
pairs.but_this: Enter your password again to continue.
pairs.rationale: Atlassian: skip 'just' in error states. Users already feel friction; reminding them it's 'just' something undermines the acknowledgment.
GOV.UK: link text must describe the destination. 'Click here' fails for screen-reader users who hear links out of context.
Primer: link the descriptive noun phrase, not the parenthetical. Meaningful link text works out-of-context.

pairs.not_this: Applicants must submit documentation prior to commencement.
pairs.but_this: Send your documents before you start.
pairs.rationale: USWDS: federal guidance — plain language serves accessibility and civic participation.

pairs.but_this: No orders yet. They'll show up here as they come in.
pairs.rationale: Polaris empty-state principles: name what's missing, set expectations, point at next action.
Polaris: empty-state CTAs should be specific — what CAN the user do right now?
Mailchimp: robotic tone undermines earned celebratory moments. Specific + warm beats procedural.
Atlassian: drop the procedural 'operation completed' — name what happened in product terms.
Microsoft: 'Yes' buttons rely on the reader remembering the question; action verbs survive standalone reading.

pairs.not_this: This app needs access to your location.
pairs.but_this: This app uses your location to suggest nearby coffee shops. You can change this in Settings.
pairs.rationale: Apple HIG: permission requests explain WHY, not just what. Trust comes from specificity.

pairs.not_this: By proceeding, you acknowledge and consent to the collection, processing, and storage of your personal information.
pairs.but_this: We'll store your email so you can sign back in. You can delete it anytime in Settings.
pairs.rationale: Carbon: plain language in legal-adjacent contexts. Specificity + action beat legalese + abstract consent.

pairs.not_this: Save up to thirty percent when you upgrade.
pairs.but_this: Save up to 30% when you upgrade.
pairs.rationale: Mailchimp + Chicago: use numerals for data (prices, percentages) so readers can scan. Spell out non-data numbers.
Primer: the canonical contraction confusion. Catching it keeps the tool credible.
Apple HIG: sentence case. Inconsistent title case (lowercase 'the', uppercase 'Next') fails visual hygiene.
Mailchimp: ALL CAPS reads as shouting and harms screen-reader pronunciation. Exception: acronyms.

pairs.source_section: Voice and tone — One idea per sentence
pairs.not_this: You can export your data as a CSV which will include all records from the selected date range and can be opened in Excel or Google Sheets for further analysis.
pairs.but_this: Export your data as CSV. The file includes all records in the date range you picked; open it in Excel or Google Sheets.
pairs.rationale: Atlassian: one idea per sentence beats clause-chain. The original has three; the rewrite has two, cleanly.

pairs.not_this: Users should enter their address here.
pairs.rationale: GOV.UK: drop third-person references to 'users' in UI strings. Talk to the reader directly.
Data-display context: the terse form IS the standard. This pair is an example where PRF-01's 'use complete sentences' suppresses in dense tabular/metric contexts.

pairs.not_this: If a applicant meet the criteria...
pairs.but_this: If an applicant meets the criteria...
pairs.rationale: USWDS: grammar errors in federal copy erode trust. Subject-verb agreement + a/an distinction.
Microsoft: don't end headings with periods. Headings aren't sentences; the period reads as a stop where the eye expects continuation.
